userStories.title: Bootstrap and validate karl workspace structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need karl to initialize and validate the required Agents, Input, and Output folders, with ADR records stored in Output/ADR, so the workflow has a predictable filesystem contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Required folders exist or are created: Agents, Input, Output, Output/ADR
Input supports prd.json and CLAUDE.md as canonical inputs
Output supports progress.md and tech.md as canonical outputs
Startup fails with a clear error when required files are missing after bootstrap
Filesystem validation behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: ADR folder moved under Output so all durable runtime artifacts live together.

userStories.title: Single-instance protection using LOCK file
userStories.description: As the karl runtime, I need to prevent concurrent runs in the same repository so that multiple instances cannot corrupt branch state, PRD state, or output artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: On startup karl checks for a LOCK file in the project root or configured workspace root
If a LOCK file exists karl prints a clear terminal warning that another instance may already be running
karl exits without starting the loop when a LOCK file exists unless an explicit override flag is used
karl creates the LOCK file when a run starts successfully
karl removes the LOCK file on clean shutdown
Stale locks can be overridden deliberately by the operator
LOCK behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implements simple local mutual exclusion using a file named LOCK.

userStories.title: Ensure git repository exists or initialize one
userStories.description: As the karl runtime, I need to verify that the project directory is a git repository so that branch-based development and commits can work. If no repository exists, karl should warn and initialize one.
acceptanceCriteria: On startup karl checks whether the current working directory contains a git repository
If no repository exists, karl prints a clear warning explaining that git is required for branch-based workflow
karl initializes a new git repository when auto-init is enabled or the operator confirms initialization
A default main branch is created if none exists
Initialization failure stops execution with a clear error message
The check occurs before any branch creation or ticket execution
Behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Required prerequisite for gitflow branch creation and commit workflow.

userStories.title: Validate Claude CLI installation before loop start
userStories.description: As a user, I want karl to verify Claude CLI is installed and callable before starting so that the loop fails fast with actionable guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: Startup checks that Claude CLI binary is installed and executable
Startup checks return a clear actionable error when Claude is missing
The loop does not begin when Claude validation fails
Validation result is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Hard failure when Claude is not installed.

userStories.title: Read Ralph-style prd.json and select highest-priority unfinished ticket
userStories.description: As the planning system, I need to parse a Ralph-style prd.json and choose the next story with the highest priority where passes=false.
acceptanceCriteria: karl reads Input/prd.json in a Ralph-compatible structure
karl selects the unfinished story with the lowest numeric priority value among passes=false items
The runtime exits cleanly with a clear message when all stories pass
Malformed PRD content produces a clear validation error
Selection behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Preserves the Ralph contract while enabling multi-agent execution.

userStories.title: Create deterministic gitflow feature branch from selected ticket
userStories.description: As the loop, I need to create and switch to a deterministic feature branch derived from the selected ticket so that each iteration is isolated and traceable in git history.
acceptanceCriteria: Selected story produces a deterministic gitflow feature branch name using the ticket id and a sanitized title
Branch format follows feature/<ticket-id>-<slug>
Given the same ticket, branch name generation always returns the same string
The branch is created from main or reused if it already exists for the active ticket
Branch creation failures stop the iteration with a clear error
Branch naming behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Deterministic branch names reduce ambiguity for agents and recovery flows.

userStories.title: Load agent registry from markdown files in Agents folder
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I need to load agent definitions from markdown files in the Agents folder so that karl can run as a multi-agent workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: karl reads markdown-based agent definitions from Agents/
Missing required agents A-F produce a clear validation error
Agent registry resolves these roles: Architect, Planner, Reviewer, Tester, Developer, Deployment
Agent prompts can be composed with ticket, plan, ADR, tech, and test context
Registry loading is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Major architectural difference from Ralph.

userStories.title: Define agent prompt contract and structured outputs
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I need each agent markdown definition to follow a clear contract for inputs, responsibilities, and output format so that multi-agent handoffs remain deterministic and parseable.
acceptanceCriteria: Each required agent markdown file defines the agent role, expected inputs, expected outputs, and constraints
Architect, Planner, Reviewer, Tester, Developer, Deployment agents each have a documented prompt contract
Agent outputs use a structured format that downstream steps can validate and consume
Prompt contract validation fails clearly when a required contract field is missing
Structured output behavior is covered by automated tests
Use the current /Agent folder as templates for defining the expected markdown structure and content for each agent role
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reduces drift and ambiguity across agent handoffs.

userStories.title: Implement planning and review loop between agents A and B
userStories.description: As the workflow, I need a planning phase where agent A creates a plan and test recommendations, and agent B reviews that plan before execution starts.
acceptanceCriteria: Planning agent A creates a structured implementation plan for the selected story
The plan explicitly includes testing recommendations
Reviewing agent B can approve or return actionable feedback on the plan
Planning agent A can incorporate review feedback and produce a revised plan
Final approved plan is persisted in Output for iteration traceability
Commits are created for plan and review artifacts to link them to git history
Workflow behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Core coordination flow before architecture, test, and implementation.

userStories.title: Implement architecture review and ADR maintenance
userStories.description: As the workflow, I need the architect agent to validate plans against ADRs and tech.md and create or update ADR records when architectural changes are required.
acceptanceCriteria: Architect agent C can read Output/ADR/*.md and Output/tech.md
Architect agent determines whether the selected change requires a new ADR or an update to an existing ADR
ADR changes are written in a consistent format and stored in Output/ADR
Architect output is traceable to the active ticket
No ADR change is created when the current architecture already covers the plan
Commits are created if changes are made to ADR records or tech.md to link them to git history
ADR workflow is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Keeps architectural records consistent and concise.

userStories.title: Implement test-first handoff from agent D to agent E
userStories.description: As the workflow, I need the test agent to create implementation tests before development so that the developer agent works against explicit validation.
acceptanceCriteria: Test agent D generates or updates tests from the approved plan before implementation starts
Generated tests are written into the Output codebase in expected project locations
Developer agent E receives the approved plan and test context
Commits are created if tests are added or updated to link them to git history
The workflow records which tests were introduced or changed for the ticket
Test generation flow is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Encodes the intended test-first behavior.

userStories.title: Implement configurable per-ticket iteration retry limit
userStories.description: As an operator, I need the developer and test rework loop to stop after a configurable number of retries so that karl does not get stuck indefinitely on a single ticket.
acceptanceCriteria: karl accepts a command line parameter for the maximum number of rework cycles per ticket
The default maximum is 10 when the parameter is not provided
The retry counter applies to the developer and tester rework loop for a single active ticket
When the limit is reached the iteration stops with a clear terminal message and durable artifact explaining the failure
The configured retry limit is visible in runtime logs for the active ticket
Retry-limit behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implements timeout protection as a configurable runtime parameter rather than a hardcoded constant.

userStories.title: Implement developer and test rework loop until green or limit reached
userStories.description: As the workflow, I need the developer and test agents to iterate until tests are green, while allowing the test agent to fix incorrect tests when appropriate.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent E implements the selected story based on the approved plan
Agent D runs tests after implementation and reports failures back to agent E
When a failure is caused by an incorrect test, agent D can correct the test and rerun it
When a failure is caused by implementation, agent E updates the code and reruns the checks
The iteration stops only when all required tests for the ticket are green or the configured retry limit is reached
On completion a commit is created that includes the final implementation and test changes linked to the active ticket
Rework-loop behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Critical for autonomous multi-agent convergence.

userStories.title: Implement safe merge policy before merging to main
userStories.description: As the deployment workflow, I need to verify repository safety conditions before merging so that autonomous branch management does not leave the repository in a broken state.
acceptanceCriteria: Before merge karl verifies the working tree is clean except for intended committed changes
All open changes made by previous agents are committed before merge
Before merge karl verifies main exists and is checked against the latest local state
Before merge karl verifies the active feature branch can merge or rebase cleanly onto main
If merge conflicts occur karl tries to resolve them automatically or stops with a clear error message if it cannot
Merge checks are logged for the active ticket
Merge behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Separates merge safety from general deployment quality gates.

userStories.title: Run deployment gate and enforce quality requirements
userStories.description: As the deployment agent, I need to verify that tests, quality gates, and ADR expectations are satisfied before code is committed and merged.
acceptanceCriteria: Deployment agent F verifies all required tests pass
Deployment agent F verifies configured quality gates pass, including typecheck and project-defined checks
Deployment agent F validates implementation consistency with relevant ADRs
If any gate fails, the ticket is sent back to the developer workflow instead of merging
Gate decisions are logged for the active ticket and a commit is created for any gate-related changes to link them to git history
Deployment-gate behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Final release control step of each iteration.

userStories.title: Commit, merge to main, and update PRD and progress after successful iteration
userStories.description: As the workflow, I need successful work to be committed, merged, and recorded so that future iterations inherit durable state from git history, progress.md, and prd.json.
acceptanceCriteria: After a successful iteration, all changes are committed with a message that includes the ticket id and summary
Selected story is updated to passes=true in Input/prd.json after merge success
Output/progress.md is appended with a concise iteration summary and learnings
The feature branch is cleaned up after merge
Commit-and-merge behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Persists durable memory similarly to Ralph.

userStories.title: Persist structured iteration artifacts for traceability
userStories.description: As an operator, I need karl to persist plan, review, architecture, test, and deployment artifacts in a structured directory layout so that each ticket execution can be audited and resumed safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Output contains structured artifact directories for plan, review, architecture, tests, implementation, and deployment records
Each iteration stores structured artifacts in Output linked to the active ticket id
Output/ADR is used as the canonical ADR location
Artifact persistence is append-safe and does not overwrite unrelated ticket history
Stored artifacts are concise enough for repeated agent reuse
Artifact-persistence behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Adds the structured artifact format you requested.

userStories.title: Apply artifact summarization policy for fresh-agent iterations
userStories.description: As the workflow, I need durable artifacts to be summarized before storage so that each new ticket iteration can start with fresh agents and a concise context footprint similar to Ralph.
acceptanceCriteria: Plans are summarized before being stored as durable artifacts
Review feedback is summarized into concise actionable output
Test results are summarized into concise status and failure context
Only information required for future file-based reuse is retained in durable artifacts
Summaries preserve references to source artifacts or filenames where deeper detail exists
Summarization behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Supports the clarified model of fresh agents with no cross-ticket context reuse.

userStories.title: Keep terminal loop running continuously
userStories.description: As a user, I want karl to continue running iteration after iteration until interrupted so that it behaves as a persistent autonomous loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Starting karl from the terminal begins a continuous loop
After a successful ticket completion, karl automatically selects the next highest-priority unfinished ticket
When no unfinished tickets remain, karl exits cleanly with a clear status message
Unexpected iteration errors are logged without corrupting PRD state
Loop-control behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Main runtime behavior requested by the user.

userStories.title: Handle Claude rate-limit responses by waiting and resuming
userStories.description: As a user, I want karl to detect Claude rate-limit responses, wait until reset, and then continue so that the loop can run unattended for long periods.
acceptanceCriteria: karl detects Claude CLI responses indicating usage or rate limits were reached
When reset timing can be determined, karl waits until the reset point before retrying
When reset timing cannot be determined, karl falls back to a configurable backoff strategy
Rate-limit events are logged without losing iteration state
Resume behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Required for non-ending loop behavior with Claude.

userStories.title: Generate first-run tech.md questionnaire and recommendations
userStories.description: As a user starting karl for the first time, I want an agent to inspect the PRD and ask concise technology questions with recommendations so that tech.md can be created deliberately.
acceptanceCriteria: On first run without Output/tech.md, karl triggers a tech-discovery flow before normal ticket execution
An agent inspects Input/prd.json and asks clear focused technology questions
The questions include recommendations rather than only open prompts
The resulting Output/tech.md is concise and optimized for low context-window usage
Subsequent runs reuse the existing tech.md unless explicitly refreshed
First-run tech-discovery behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Concise tech decisions generated from a first-run interaction.

userStories.title: Provide concise CLI logging and operator controls
userStories.description: As an operator, I want clear terminal output and basic run controls so that I can understand what karl is doing without wasting context or attention.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI output shows current ticket, current agent phase, branch name, retry count, and final iteration result
Logs clearly distinguish normal progress, warnings, and blocking failures
A dry-run mode can validate setup and show the next ticket without modifying code
A configurable max-retries or max-rework setting exists for failed iterations
CLI-control behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Operationally useful, but not foundational.

userStories.title: Add karl clean command for repository recovery
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a karl clean command that resets the repository to a safe baseline after a broken ticket implementation so that development can continue from a clean state.
acceptanceCriteria: karl exposes a clean command through the CLI, for example karl clean
The clean command clears uncommitted changes in the working tree after explicit safety confirmation or force flag
The clean command checks out main
The clean command deletes the previously active feature branch when it is safe to do so
The clean command removes stale runtime lock state and leaves the workspace in a clean recoverable state
The clean command prints clear terminal output describing what was cleaned and what remains
Clean-command behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Provides an operator recovery path when autonomous work leaves the repo in a bad state.

userStories.title: Provide open-source onboarding: example workspace, README, and visual project overview
userStories.description: As an open-source user, I want karl to include a comprehensive README, example project workspace, and visual architecture overview so that developers can quickly understand how to install, configure, and run karl without reading internal code.
acceptanceCriteria: Repository contains a comprehensive README.md explaining the purpose of karl and how it relates to the Ralph-style development loop
README includes installation instructions for prerequisites such as Claude CLI and git
README explains the folder structure: Agents, Input, Output, Output/ADR, and example artifacts
README documents the multi-agent workflow including planner, reviewer, architect, tester, developer, and deployment agents
README documents CLI commands including run, clean, and configuration parameters such as retry limits
README includes a visually appealing architecture diagram illustrating the karl loop and multi-agent workflow
Repository includes an example workspace with sample Agents markdown files, Input/prd.json, CLAUDE.md, and Output directory structure
Example prd.json demonstrates at least one simple feature ticket so users can see how karl operates
Documentation explains how to extend agents and modify prompts
All documentation renders correctly on GitHub
Documentation quality and example workspace are covered by automated validation where feasible
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Ensures the project is usable as an open-source tool. The README should include a diagram (e.g., SVG or PNG) showing the karl multi-agent loop.

userStories.title: Reduce logging and add parameter to enable verbose mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want karl to have concise logging by default and an option to enable verbose logging so that I can choose the level of detail I see in the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: By default, karl logs only high-level progress and important events to the terminal
karl accepts a command line parameter (e.g., --verbose) that enables detailed logging of agent outputs, decisions, and internal state changes
Verbose logging includes timestamps and ticket identifiers for easier debugging
The logging level can be changed without modifying code, only through CLI parameters
Logging behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Improves usability by reducing noise in the terminal while still allowing access to detailed logs when needed for debugging or insight into agent behavior.

userStories.title: Implement a multi instance version of karl that supports multiple concurrent loops with isolated state
userStories.description: As an advanced user, I want karl to support multiple concurrent instances with isolated state so that I can run separate loops in the same repository without interference.
acceptanceCriteria: karl can be configured to run multiple instances simultaneously with isolated state and branch management
Each instance uses a unique identifier to manage its own LOCK file, branches, and output artifacts
Ensure that no instances are operating on the same ticket or branch to prevent state corruption
Instances can run independently without interfering with each other's state or branches
Documentation explains how to set up and manage multiple karl instances in the same repository
By default this is not enabled to avoid complexity for new users, but can be turned on with a CLI parameter (e.g., --instances <number>)
The dashboard displays the status of all active instances when multi-instance mode is enabled
Multi-instance behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Provides resilience against unexpected issues and allows users to pause and resume work without losing progress.
